steps.action: Tinikling (traditionally written tiniclín) is a with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Tinikling (traditionally written tiniclín) is a with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: The dance involves at least two people beating,
steps.text: The dance involves at least two people beating, tapping, and sliding bamboo poles on the ground and against each other in coordination with one or more dancers who step over and in between the poles in a dance.

steps.action: It is traditionally danced to rondalla music, a
steps.text: It is traditionally danced to rondalla music, a sort of serenade played by an ensemble of stringed instruments which originated in Spain during the Middle Ages.

steps.action: The locomotor movements used in this dance are h
steps.text: The locomotor movements used in this dance are hopping, jumping, and turning.

steps.action: "The dance originated in Palo, Leyte, Island in
steps.text: "The dance originated in Palo, Leyte, Island in the Visayas.

steps.action: Dancers imitate the tikling bird's legendary gra
steps.text: Dancers imitate the tikling bird's legendary grace and speed by skillfully maneuvering between large bamboo poles.

steps.action: However, other known stories also explain where
steps.text: However, other known stories also explain where this national dance originated from.

steps.action: A more popular one is the legend, without histor
steps.text: A more popular one is the legend, without historical evidence, that says the tinikling dance originated from Filipino field workers during the time of Spanish colonization.

steps.action: What was once a way to avoid punishment now beca
steps.text: What was once a way to avoid punishment now became a form of art and dance.

steps.action: In grades K-12 the dance is used as an aerobic e
steps.text: In grades K-12 the dance is used as an aerobic exercise for physical education classes, to help expand physical movements such as hand coordination, foot speed, and also rhythm.

steps.action: Tinikling is commonly performed at schools and o
steps.text: Tinikling is commonly performed at schools and on special occasions, such as the Filipino Independence Day, as a celebration of Filipino culture and Filipino pride.

steps.action: Traditionally, the poles are tapped twice on the
steps.text: Traditionally, the poles are tapped twice on the ground on the first two beats, then brought together on the third beat.

steps.action: Two or more dancers then weave through the rapid
steps.text: Two or more dancers then weave through the rapidly moving bamboo poles with bare feet and ankles.

steps.action: The dancers have to carefully follow the rhythm
steps.text: The dancers have to carefully follow the rhythm so as not to get their ankles caught between the poles as they snap closed.

steps.action: They start the dance with their hands at their h
steps.text: They start the dance with their hands at their hips or clasped behind their backs.

steps.action: The tempo of the bamboo poles becomes faster as
steps.text: The tempo of the bamboo poles becomes faster as the dance progresses, forcing the dancers closer together as their movements become more frantic.

steps.action: The dancers hold hands at the last part of the d
steps.text: The dancers hold hands at the last part of the dance when the tempo is the fastest.

steps.action: They end the dance by letting go of each other's
steps.text: They end the dance by letting go of each other's hands and stepping out entirely of the moving bamboo poles.

steps.action: For the dance, women traditionally wear a dress
steps.text: For the dance, women traditionally wear a dress called Balintawak or patadyong, and men wear an untucked embroidered shirt called the barong Tagalog.

steps.action: Dancers wear no footwear while performing.
steps.text: Dancers wear no footwear while performing.

steps.action: Tinikling has also been noted to have the music
steps.text: Tinikling has also been noted to have the music changed in modern times to modern songs with strong percussions and bass to connect the traditions of the Philippine folk dance with their modern-day lifestyle.

steps.action: In the Philippines, the dance is often performed
steps.text: In the Philippines, the dance is often performed on certain Sundays.

steps.action: These dances originate mostly from the islands o
steps.text: These dances originate mostly from the islands of Visayas and imitate the simplicity and joy of the lifestyle of the Filipino villagers living in those regions during the Spanish period.

steps.action: Other Filipino folk dances of this category incl
steps.text: Other Filipino folk dances of this category include Sayaw sa Bangko, Maglalatik, and Pandanggo sa Ilaw.
